questions.q: Which committee of the Constituent Assembly did Dr. B.R. Ambedkar chair?
questions.explanation: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar chaired the Drafting Committee of the Constituent Assembly.

questions.q: What is the full name of Dr. B.R. Ambedkar?
questions.explanation: His full name was Bhimrao Ramji Ambedkar.

questions.q: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar is widely regarded as the principal architect of which document?
options: The Government of India Act of 1935
questions.explanation: Dr. Ambedkar is widely regarded as the principal architect of the Constitution of India.

questions.q: Which cabinet post did Dr. B.R. Ambedkar hold in independent India's first government?
questions.explanation: Dr. Ambedkar served as independent India's first Law Minister.

questions.q: In which year is Dr. B.R. Ambedkar's birth anniversary observed each year?
questions.explanation: Dr. Ambedkar was born on 14 April 1891, observed annually as Ambedkar Jayanti.

questions.q: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was a strong advocate for the rights of which sections of society?
questions.explanation: Dr. Ambedkar championed the rights of marginalised and depressed classes throughout his life.

questions.q: India's highest civilian award was conferred on Dr. B.R. Ambedkar. Which award was it?
questions.explanation: Dr. Ambedkar was posthumously conferred the Bharat Ratna, India's highest civilian award.

questions.q: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was a member of which assembly that framed the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Dr. Ambedkar was a member of the Constituent Assembly of India.

questions.q: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was a scholar widely known for his expertise in which fields?
questions.explanation: Dr. Ambedkar was a distinguished scholar of law and economics.

questions.q: A grand national memorial to Dr. Ambedkar is associated with which water body in Mumbai?
options: Chaitya Bhoomi by the Arabian Sea
questions.explanation: Chaitya Bhoomi, the memorial to Dr. Ambedkar, is located by the Arabian Sea in Mumbai.

questions.q: In which year was Dr. B.R. Ambedkar born?
questions.explanation: Dr. Ambedkar was born on 14 April 1891.

questions.q: In which year did Dr. B.R. Ambedkar pass away?
questions.explanation: Dr. Ambedkar passed away on 6 December 1956.

questions.q: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar earned doctoral degrees from which two renowned universities?
options: Columbia University and the London School of Economics
questions.explanation: Dr. Ambedkar earned doctorates from Columbia University and the London School of Economics.

questions.q: Which Marathi-language journal did Dr. B.R. Ambedkar found in 1920 early in his public life?
questions.explanation: Dr. Ambedkar founded the Marathi fortnightly Mooknayak in 1920 to voice the concerns of the depressed classes.

questions.q: In which Indian state was Dr. B.R. Ambedkar born?
options: In the town of Mhow, in present-day Madhya Pradesh
questions.explanation: Dr. Ambedkar was born in Mhow, located in present-day Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar described which article, on the right to constitutional remedies, as the 'heart and soul' of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Dr. Ambedkar called Article 32, the right to constitutional remedies, the heart and soul of the Constitution.

questions.q: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar represented the depressed classes at which series of conferences held in London in the early 1930s?
questions.explanation: Dr. Ambedkar represented the depressed classes at the Round Table Conferences in London.

questions.q: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar resigned from the Union Cabinet in which year?
questions.explanation: Dr. Ambedkar resigned from the Union Cabinet in 1951.

questions.q: Late in his life, in 1956, Dr. B.R. Ambedkar formally embraced which religion?
questions.explanation: In 1956 Dr. Ambedkar formally embraced Buddhism along with many of his followers.

questions.q: In which city did Dr. B.R. Ambedkar's mass conversion ceremony to Buddhism take place in 1956?
questions.explanation: Dr. Ambedkar's mass conversion to Buddhism took place at Nagpur in 1956.

questions.q: Which institution did Dr. B.R. Ambedkar establish in 1945 to promote higher education?
questions.explanation: Dr. Ambedkar founded the People's Education Society in 1945 to advance education among marginalised communities.

questions.q: Which 1927 movement led by Dr. Ambedkar asserted the right of all to draw water from a public tank?
questions.explanation: The Mahad Satyagraha of 1927, led by Dr. Ambedkar, asserted the right of the depressed classes to use public water sources.

questions.q: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was first elected to the Constituent Assembly from which province?
questions.explanation: Dr. Ambedkar was initially elected to the Constituent Assembly from the province of Bengal.

questions.q: Which early academic work did Dr. B.R. Ambedkar write on Indian public finance?
questions.explanation: Dr. Ambedkar wrote 'The Problem of the Rupee', an influential work on Indian currency and finance.

questions.q: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar's testimony on currency contributed to the establishment of which financial institution in 1935?
questions.explanation: Dr. Ambedkar's ideas on currency and finance contributed to the conceptual basis of the Reserve Bank of India, established in 1935.

questions.q: Which political party did Dr. B.R. Ambedkar found in 1936 in the Bombay region?
questions.explanation: Dr. Ambedkar founded the Independent Labour Party in 1936.

questions.q: Which organisation did Dr. B.R. Ambedkar establish later, in 1942, to advance the political interests of marginalised communities?
options: The All India Trade Union Congress
questions.explanation: Dr. Ambedkar founded the All India Scheduled Castes Federation in 1942.

questions.q: The 1932 Poona Pact, which Dr. Ambedkar signed, concerned which subject?
options: The abolition of the zamindari system
Reserved electoral seats for the depressed classes
questions.explanation: The Poona Pact of 1932 provided for reserved seats for the depressed classes within the general electorate.

questions.q: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar's final major literary work, published after his death, was titled:
options: Pakistan or the Partition of India
questions.explanation: His final major work, 'The Buddha and His Dhamma', was published posthumously.

questions.q: In the first general elections, Dr. Ambedkar was later elected to which House of Parliament?
options: The Legislative Council of Bombay only
He was never elected to Parliament
questions.explanation: Dr. Ambedkar later served as a member of the Rajya Sabha, the upper house of Parliament.
